question: Ran out of Garlic Bread at 8pm on a Friday. Needs better planning.
contexts: Ran out of Garlic Bread at 8pm on a Friday. Needs better planning.
Ran out of Garlic Bread again on May 29. Third Friday in a row. Unacceptable!
Garlic bread and sides must be stocked sufficiently for Friday peak — minimum 80 portions.
Garlic bread and sides must be stocked sufficiently for Friday peak — minimum 80 portions.
answer: Garlic bread and sides must be stocked sufficiently for Friday peak — minimum 80 portions. Garlic bread ran out at 8pm on a Friday. It ran out again on May 29 — the third Friday in a row.
ground_truth: Stock a minimum of 80 portions of garlic bread and sides for Friday peak. Running out on three consecutive Fridays is a recurring planning failure.

question: Pasta at 1pm — waited 35 minutes for a simple Carbonara. Office lunch ruined.
contexts: Pasta at 1pm — waited 35 minutes for a simple Carbonara. Office lunch ruined.
May 22 service was chaos — waited 50 minutes for a pizza that came out cold.
Fast lunch pacing matters more than broad menu experimentation.
Takeaway packaging, handoff speed, and cashier flow are high-sensitivity points.
answer: Fast lunch pacing matters more than broad menu experimentation. Handoff speed and cashier flow are high-sensitivity points. Guests waited 35 minutes for a Carbonara at 1pm and 50 minutes for a pizza on May 22, which came out cold.
ground_truth: Fast lunch pacing is the priority. Handoff speed and cashier flow are high-sensitivity points. 35-minute pasta wait and 50-minute pizza wait show the service window is not meeting pace expectations.

question: The Pepperoni Feast was cold when it arrived. Very disappointing.
contexts: The Pepperoni Feast was cold when it arrived. Very disappointing.

answer: Quality slippage under heavy volume is more damaging than small menu restraint. Queue growth and delayed dispatch will be amplified under surge demand. The Pepperoni Feast arrived cold, and on May 22 a pizza arrived cold after a 50-minute wait during a chaotic service.
ground_truth: Quality slippage under heavy volume is more damaging than menu restraint. Surge demand amplifies queue growth and delayed dispatch. Cold food on arrival is a direct output of these conditions.

question: Large group of 8 — seating took forever. No coordination from front of house.
contexts: Large group of 8 — seating took forever. No coordination from front of house.
Caesar Salad came without croutons. Small thing but frustrating for a quick lunch.
Fast lunch pacing matters more than broad menu experimentation.

answer: Fast lunch pacing matters more than broad menu experimentation. Handoff speed and cashier flow are high-sensitivity points. A group of 8 reported that seating took a long time with no front-of-house coordination, and a Caesar Salad arrived without croutons.
ground_truth: Fast pacing and handoff speed are high-sensitivity for weekday lunch. Poor front-of-house coordination for large groups and missing items like croutons both signal execution gaps.

question: Holiday Monday and you ran out of Mango Smoothie by 7pm. Disappointing.
contexts: Holiday Monday and you ran out of Mango Smoothie by 7pm. Disappointing.
Ran out of Garlic Bread at 8pm on a Friday. Needs better planning.

answer: Queue growth and delayed dispatch will be amplified under surge demand. Quality slippage under heavy volume is more damaging than small menu restraint. The restaurant ran out of Mango Smoothie by 7pm on a Holiday Monday. Garlic Bread also ran out at 8pm on a Friday.
ground_truth: Surge demand amplifies queue growth. Stock high-demand items like Mango Smoothie and Garlic Bread above normal capacity for holiday service to prevent stockouts.
